steps.title: Identify the scoring basis
steps.content: The underlined sentence says the six-point scale considers varying levels of supporting evidence. The examples that follow show species receiving different scores because the evidence for bioluminescence varies in strength.

steps.title: Reject broader functions
steps.content: Choice A shifts to researcher thoroughness. Choice B says the sentence explains how the system came from the old binary system, which it does not. Choice C points to the need for a new system, but that was established earlier. The underlined sentence explains what the scores are based on. The answer is D.
